text: The average increase in VO2max after 20 weeks of standardized exercise training in sedentary adults is approximately 400 ml/min.
Individual VO2max training responses are highly heterogeneous, ranging from little or no gain to increases exceeding 1.0 l/min on the same standardized program.
There is 2.5 times more variance between families than within families in VO2max response to training, indicating strong familial aggregation of trainability.
The heritability of VO2max trainability is estimated at 47% after adjusting for age and sex.
Maternal transmission accounts for an estimated 28% of the familial component of VO2max trainability, suggesting possible mitochondrial DNA or maternal environmental influences.
Comparing an individual's training response to population averages is unreliable because genetic factors account for nearly half of the variation in VO2max trainability.
